epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Urgency with or without urge incontinence for >3 months; exclude infection; bladder diary

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; adherence to behavioral therapy varies

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and urology evaluation; rule out UTI
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Behavioral therapy, pelvic floor training, antimuscarinics (oxybutynin) via SUS; mirabegron in select centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited urogynecology access and medication adherence
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care access and low-cost antimuscarinics
